title: Vitakka/vicƒÅra gloss-shift: dating the "application" (abhiniropana) reading

hypotheses.h1: HOUSE THESIS (canon supple, commentary systematizes). Within passages that co-mention vitakka/vicƒÅra, the APPLICATION lexicon (abhiniropana and kin) is rare and non-definitional in the canonical m≈´la layer (work_role='mula', work_slug<>'pli-vism'), where vitakka sits inside an open discursive/intentional field (sa·πÖkappa, takka, papa√±ca, cintƒÅ). The APPLICATION reading hardens in commentary: the within-vitakka APPLICATION/DISCURSIVE per-Mchar ratio rises monotonically mula -> attha(+pli-vism) -> tika, and abhiniropana acquires definitional-head-noun status (co-occurring with ti vuccati / nƒÅma / attho / lakkha·πáa) FIRST in the a·π≠·π≠hakathƒÅ layer (AtthasƒÅlinƒ´ Dhs-a, Visuddhimagga IV), not in mula. The signal is the shape of the within-vitakka ratio across layers, NOT the stratum label.
hypotheses.h0: PRE-COMMITTED NULL (report if it wins). There is no layer-wise hardening: the within-vitakka APPLICATION/DISCURSIVE per-Mchar ratio is flat (or non-monotonic) across mula/attha/tika once length is controlled, AND any apparent rise is matched by an identical rise on the pƒ´ti negative control (i.e. a generic commentary register/length artifact, not a vitakka-specific gloss shift). Under H0 the defining-frame harvest also finds abhiniropana-as-definitional-head already present in the mula layer at a density indistinguishable from commentary. If the pƒ´ti control moves with vitakka, the effect is declared an artifact regardless of the vitakka numbers.

frozen_lexica.name: TARGET_vitakka_vicara
frozen_lexica.role: anchor term (the gloss subject); all ratios are computed WITHIN passages containing this
frozen_lexica.notes: vitakk- covers vitakka/vitakko/vitakkƒÅ/vitakka·πÉ; vicƒÅr- covers vicƒÅra/vicƒÅro/vicƒÅra·πáa/vicƒÅre; sa-/a- prefixed jhƒÅna-factor compounds included because the gloss frames cluster at savitakka savicƒÅra. \m word-boundary avoids matching inside unrelated stems. takka is a substring of vitakka and is handled by a separate \mtakk boundary in the discursive set.

frozen_lexica.name: APPLICATION
frozen_lexica.role: the 'application' commentarial gloss field; H1 predicts this hardens in commentary
frozen_lexica.notes: abhiniropan(a/ƒÅ) is the AtthasƒÅlinƒ´/Vism head gloss for vitakka; ƒÅropan/abhiropan are simplex sandhi variants in running glosses; anumajjan + anusa√±cara·πá/anusa·πÉcara·πá (√± vs niggahƒ´ta ·πÉ) + vicara·πá are the vicƒÅra gloss verbs (Vism IV.88 anusa√±cara·πáa); upanijjhƒÅn(a) is the jhƒÅna-contemplation gloss (jjh cluster; upanijhƒÅn is the common single-j mis-spelling, kept to catch dirty OCR); ƒÅramma·πáe pati·π≠·π≠hƒÅpana = establishing-on-the-object with ·π≠·π≠h cluster and short/long-a variant. DIACRITIC-CRITICAL: ·π≠·π≠h, ·πá, ƒÅ, √±, ·πÉ all load-bearing; ASCII-folded forms return ZERO.

frozen_lexica.name: DISCURSIVE
frozen_lexica.role: the open discursive/intentional field; H1 predicts vitakka sits here in mula
frozen_lexica.notes: papa√±c + niggahƒ´ta variant papa·πÉc; sa·πÖkapp + sa·πÉkapp + magga compounds micchƒÅ/sammƒÅ-sa·πÖkappa; \mtakk word-boundary is ESSENTIAL because takka is a substring of vitakka and sa·πÖkappa-takka, so without \m every vitakka double-counts as takka; cintƒÅ/cintana (thinking) and cetanƒÅ (volition, the intentional pole). \m on each prevents substring bleed. ·πÖ vs ·πÉ and √± are diacritic-critical.

frozen_lexica.name: DEFINING_FRAME
frozen_lexica.role: gloss-frame marker for the defining-frame harvest (when abhiniropana becomes a DEFINITIONAL head-noun for vitakka)
frozen_lexica.notes: TheravƒÅda commentary definitional skeleton: X ti vuccati / X nƒÅma / attho (the meaning is) / lakkha·πáa (characteristic) / rasa (function) / paccupa·π≠·π≠hƒÅna (manifestation) / kicca. A row counts as a DEFINING-FRAME hit for vitakka only when TARGET + APPLICATION + DEFINING_FRAME co-occur in one passage (coded blind, see iaa_plan). nƒÅma/attho/rasa carry \M trailing boundary to avoid matching inside longer words.

frozen_lexica.name: NEGCTRL_piti
frozen_lexica.role: negative control: a stable jhƒÅna factor that must show NO application/discursive shift
frozen_lexica.notes: pƒ´ti co-occurs with vitakka/vicƒÅra as a jhƒÅna factor but is NOT the subject of an application-gloss debate, so its APPLICATION/DISCURSIVE ratio across layers is the artifact baseline. \mpƒ´ti\M plus the listed compounds AVOID false hits on pƒ´ta (yellow), pƒ´tha (bench), and apƒ´ti, which would silently inflate the control. The long ƒ´ is diacritic-critical.

enumeration_sql.label: Q0_layer_char_denominators_sanity
enumeration_sql.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN work_role='mula' AND work_slug<>'pli-vism' THEN 'mula' WHEN (work_slug LIKE '%-attha' OR work_slug='pli-vism') THEN 'attha' WHEN work_slug LIKE '%-tika' THEN 'tika' ELSE 'other' END AS layer, count(*) AS rows, sum(char_length(original))/1e6 AS mchar FROM passages WHERE is_primary GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1;

enumeration_sql.label: Q1_target_base_rate_per_layer
enumeration_sql.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN work_role='mula' AND work_slug<>'pli-vism' THEN 'mula' WHEN (work_slug LIKE '%-attha' OR work_slug='pli-vism') THEN 'attha' WHEN work_slug LIKE '%-tika' THEN 'tika' ELSE 'other' END AS layer, count(*) AS target_rows, sum(char_length(original)) AS target_chars FROM passages WHERE is_primary AND original ~* '\m(vitakk|savitakk|avitakk|vicƒÅr|savicƒÅr|avicƒÅr)' GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1;

enumeration_sql.label: Q2_within_target_application_vs_discursive_density
enumeration_sql.sql: WITH t AS (SELECT *, CASE WHEN work_role='mula' AND work_slug<>'pli-vism' THEN 'mula' WHEN (work_slug LIKE '%-attha' OR work_slug='pli-vism') THEN 'attha' WHEN work_slug LIKE '%-tika' THEN 'tika' ELSE 'other' END AS layer FROM passages WHERE is_primary AND original ~* '\m(vitakk|savitakk|avitakk|vicƒÅr|savicƒÅr|avicƒÅr)') SELECT layer, count(*) AS target_rows, sum(char_length(original))/1e6 AS target_mchar, count(*) FILTER (WHERE original ~* 'abhiniropan|abhiropan|ƒÅropan|anuropan|anumajjan|anusa√±cara·πá|anusa·πÉcara·πá|vicara·πá|upanijjhƒÅn|upanijhƒÅn|pati·π≠·π≠hƒÅpan|pati·π≠·π≠hapan') AS app_rows, count(*) FILTER (WHERE original ~* 'papa√±c|papa·πÉc|\msa·πÖkapp|\msa·πÉkapp|\mtakk|\mcintƒÅ|\mcintan|\mcetan') AS disc_rows, round((count(*) FILTER (WHERE original ~* 'abhiniropan|abhiropan|ƒÅropan|anuropan|anumajjan|anusa√±cara·πá|anusa·πÉcara·πá|vicara·πá|upanijjhƒÅn|upanijhƒÅn|pati·π≠·π≠hƒÅpan|pati·π≠·π≠hapan'))::numeric / (sum(char_length(original))/1e6),3) AS app_per_mchar, round((count(*) FILTER (WHERE original ~* 'papa√±c|papa·πÉc|\msa·πÖkapp|\msa·πÉkapp|\mtakk|\mcintƒÅ|\mcintan|\mcetan'))::numeric / (sum(char_length(original))/1e6),3) AS disc_per_mchar FROM t GROUP BY layer ORDER BY layer;

enumeration_sql.label: Q3_application_vs_discursive_RATIO_shape
enumeration_sql.sql: WITH t AS (SELECT CASE WHEN work_role='mula' AND work_slug<>'pli-vism' THEN 'mula' WHEN (work_slug LIKE '%-attha' OR work_slug='pli-vism') THEN 'attha' WHEN work_slug LIKE '%-tika' THEN 'tika' ELSE 'other' END AS layer, (original ~* 'abhiniropan|abhiropan|ƒÅropan|anuropan|anumajjan|anusa√±cara·πá|anusa·πÉcara·πá|vicara·πá|upanijjhƒÅn|upanijhƒÅn|pati·π≠·π≠hƒÅpan|pati·π≠·π≠hapan') AS app, (original ~* 'papa√±c|papa·πÉc|\msa·πÖkapp|\msa·πÉkapp|\mtakk|\mcintƒÅ|\mcintan|\mcetan') AS disc FROM passages WHERE is_primary AND original ~* '\m(vitakk|savitakk|avitakk|vicƒÅr|savicƒÅr|avicƒÅr)') SELECT layer, count(*) FILTER (WHERE app) AS app_rows, count(*) FILTER (WHERE disc) AS disc_rows, round(count(*) FILTER (WHERE app)::numeric / NULLIF(count(*) FILTER (WHERE disc),0),3) AS app_over_disc_ratio FROM t GROUP BY layer ORDER BY CASE layer WHEN 'mula' THEN 1 WHEN 'attha' THEN 2 WHEN 'tika' THEN 3 ELSE 4 END;

enumeration_sql.label: Q4_NEGCTRL_piti_same_ratio_shape
enumeration_sql.sql: WITH t AS (SELECT CASE WHEN work_role='mula' AND work_slug<>'pli-vism' THEN 'mula' WHEN (work_slug LIKE '%-attha' OR work_slug='pli-vism') THEN 'attha' WHEN work_slug LIKE '%-tika' THEN 'tika' ELSE 'other' END AS layer, (original ~* 'abhiniropan|abhiropan|ƒÅropan|anuropan|anumajjan|anusa√±cara·πá|anusa·πÉcara·πá|vicara·πá|upanijjhƒÅn|upanijhƒÅn|pati·π≠·π≠hƒÅpan|pati·π≠·π≠hapan') AS app, (original ~* 'papa√±c|papa·πÉc|\msa·πÖkapp|\msa·πÉkapp|\mtakk|\mcintƒÅ|\mcintan|\mcetan') AS disc FROM passages WHERE is_primary AND original ~* '\mpƒ´ti\M|\mpƒ´tiy|\mpƒ´tisukh|\msappƒ´tik|\mnippƒ´tik') SELECT layer, count(*) AS piti_rows, count(*) FILTER (WHERE app) AS app_rows, count(*) FILTER (WHERE disc) AS disc_rows, round(count(*) FILTER (WHERE app)::numeric / NULLIF(count(*) FILTER (WHERE disc),0),3) AS app_over_disc_ratio FROM t GROUP BY layer ORDER BY CASE layer WHEN 'mula' THEN 1 WHEN 'attha' THEN 2 WHEN 'tika' THEN 3 ELSE 4 END;

enumeration_sql.label: Q5_defining_frame_rows_to_code
enumeration_sql.sql: SELECT id, work_slug, work_role, CASE WHEN work_role='mula' AND work_slug<>'pli-vism' THEN 'mula' WHEN (work_slug LIKE '%-attha' OR work_slug='pli-vism') THEN 'attha' WHEN work_slug LIKE '%-tika' THEN 'tika' ELSE 'other' END AS layer, char_length(original) AS clen, substring(original from 1 for 1200) AS original_head FROM passages WHERE is_primary AND original ~* '\m(vitakk|savitakk|avitakk)' AND original ~* 'abhiniropan|abhiropan|ƒÅropan|anuropan' AND original ~* '\mti[[:space:]]+vuccati|\mnƒÅma\M|\mattho\M|lakkha·πá|\mvuccati\M|paccupa·π≠·π≠hƒÅn|\mrasa\M|kicc' ORDER BY layer, work_slug, id;

enumeration_sql.label: Q6_first_definitional_locus_by_work
enumeration_sql.sql: SELECT work_slug, CASE WHEN work_role='mula' AND work_slug<>'pli-vism' THEN 'mula' WHEN (work_slug LIKE '%-attha' OR work_slug='pli-vism') THEN 'attha' WHEN work_slug LIKE '%-tika' THEN 'tika' ELSE 'other' END AS layer, count(*) AS defframe_hits FROM passages WHERE is_primary AND original ~* '\m(vitakk|savitakk|avitakk)' AND original ~* 'abhiniropan|abhiropan|ƒÅropan|anuropan' AND original ~* '\mti[[:space:]]+vuccati|\mnƒÅma\M|\mattho\M|lakkha·πá|paccupa·π≠·π≠hƒÅn|\mrasa\M' GROUP BY work_slug, layer ORDER BY layer, defframe_hits DESC;

enumeration_sql.label: Q7_primary_loci_targeted_pull
enumeration_sql.sql: SELECT id, work_slug, work_role, char_length(original) AS clen, substring(original from 1 for 1500) AS original_head FROM passages WHERE is_primary AND (work_slug ~* 'mn0?117|mn0?019|mn0?020|mn0?078|dhs|pli-vism|patis|vibh') AND original ~* '\m(vitakk|vicƒÅr)' AND original ~* 'abhiniropan|abhiropan|ƒÅropan|anumajjan|anusa√±cara·πá|upanijjhƒÅn|takko[[:space:]]+vitakko' ORDER BY work_slug, id;

enumeration_sql.label: Q8_negctrl_piti_defining_frame_should_be_empty_or_flat
enumeration_sql.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN work_role='mula' AND work_slug<>'pli-vism' THEN 'mula' WHEN (work_slug LIKE '%-attha' OR work_slug='pli-vism') THEN 'attha' WHEN work_slug LIKE '%-tika' THEN 'tika' ELSE 'other' END AS layer, count(*) AS piti_defframe_app_rows FROM passages WHERE is_primary AND original ~* '\mpƒ´ti\M|\mpƒ´tiy|\mpƒ´tisukh' AND original ~* 'abhiniropan|abhiropan|ƒÅropan|anumajjan|upanijjhƒÅn|pati·π≠·π≠hƒÅpan' AND original ~* '\mti[[:space:]]+vuccati|\mnƒÅma\M|\mattho\M|lakkha·πá|paccupa·π≠·π≠hƒÅn' GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1;

enumeration_sql.label: Q9_lexicon_zero_guard_diacritic_check
enumeration_sql.sql: SELECT 'abhiniropan' AS term, count(*) FROM passages WHERE is_primary AND original ~* 'abhiniropan' UNION ALL SELECT 'anumajjan', count(*) FROM passages WHERE is_primary AND original ~* 'anumajjan' UNION ALL SELECT 'upanijjhan', count(*) FROM passages WHERE is_primary AND original ~* 'upanijjhƒÅn' UNION ALL SELECT 'patitthapan', count(*) FROM passages WHERE is_primary AND original ~* 'pati·π≠·π≠hƒÅpan' UNION ALL SELECT 'papanc', count(*) FROM passages WHERE is_primary AND original ~* 'papa√±c' UNION ALL SELECT 'sankapp', count(*) FROM passages WHERE is_primary AND original ~* '\msa·πÖkapp' UNION ALL SELECT 'piti', count(*) FROM passages WHERE is_primary AND original ~* '\mpƒ´ti\M';

density_method: All magnitudes are per-character, never raw rows (T2). Layers: mula = work_role='mula' AND work_slug<>'pli-vism'; attha = work_slug LIKE '%-attha' OR work_slug='pli-vism'; tika = work_slug LIKE '%-tika'. Primary metric is the WITHIN-TARGET co-occurrence density: among is_primary passages containing a TARGET (vitakka/vicƒÅra) hit, count passages also containing an APPLICATION hit and passages also containing a DISCURSIVE hit, dividing each by that layer's TARGET-subset Mchar (sum(char_length(original))/1e6 over the target subset, computed live in Q2, NOT the global per-layer denominators, because the denominator here is the target subset). The load-bearing statistic (Q3) is the within-target APPLICATION/DISCURSIVE RATIO and its SHAPE across mula->attha->tika (H1: monotone rise). Global per-layer Mchar denominators (mula 53.543921 / attha 29.488533 / tika 28.357746) are used only for the absolute base-rate normalization in Q1. is_primary dedupes the SC/CST double-ingest. Counting is passage-presence (one hit per passage), not raw stem frequency, because raw stem counts are register-confounded and the polysemy audit operates at passage granularity. Significance by bootstrap over target passages within layer; report ratio point estimate plus 95 percent CI, not a p-value alone."
negative_controls: PRIMARY: pƒ´ti (Q4), a co-present stable jhƒÅna factor NOT subject to an application-gloss debate. Its within-factor APPLICATION/DISCURSIVE ratio across mula/attha/tika is the artifact baseline. If pƒ´ti's ratio rises with the same slope as vitakka's, the vitakka rise is a generic commentary register/length artifact and H0 wins regardless of the vitakka numbers.

pƒ´ti defining-frame control (Q8): abhiniropana-style head-noun glossing must NOT attach to pƒ´ti at commentary density; a non-empty, layer-rising pƒ´ti defframe count would mean the DEFINING_FRAME skeleton is just commentary boilerplate, not vitakka-specific.
Length/register control: all densities are per-Mchar over the TARGET subset, so longer commentary passages cannot inflate raw counts; Q0 + Q2 expose the denominators for audit.
takka-substring control: \mtakk word boundary in DISCURSIVE prevents every vitakka from double-counting as takka (the single largest silent inflation risk); Q9 zero-guard confirms boundaries fire.
Diacritic zero-guard (Q9): each lexicon stem must return a non-zero plausible count; a zero means an ASCII-fold typo silently nulled the term and the run is INVALID until fixed.
falsifiers: H1 FALSIFIED if Q3 shows the within-vitakka APPLICATION/DISCURSIVE ratio is FLAT or non-monotonic across mula->attha->tika (no hardening).
H1 FALSIFIED (declared artifact) if the pƒ´ti negative control (Q4) rises with a slope statistically indistinguishable from vitakka's; the effect is generic register, not gloss-specific.

H1 FALSIFIED if the defining-frame harvest (Q5/Q6) finds abhiniropana-as-definitional-head ALREADY present in the mula layer at density indistinguishable from attha; the application reading would then be canonical, not a commentarial innovation.

H1 WEAKENED if the application rise is driven entirely by pli-vism (Visuddhimagga, shelved as mula but counted as commentary here) and vanishes when pli-vism is excluded from attha; report the pli-vism-out sensitivity.
WHOLE STUDY INVALID if any Q9 zero-guard term returns 0 (silent-diacritic failure) or if blind sense-coding kappa < 0.6 on the application-sense audit (counts uninterpretable).
predicted_signal: PREDICTED (H1, to be confirmed/refuted by Q3): within-vitakka APPLICATION/DISCURSIVE ratio rises monotonically, roughly mula < attha < tika, with the largest jump at the mula->attha boundary, and abhiniropana-as-definitional-head appearing FIRST in attha (AtthasƒÅlinƒ´ Dhs-a ~157, Vism IV.88), not in mula. TO BE MEASURED (no value committed): the absolute ratio values, the exact per-Mchar densities (Q1/Q2), whether the rise survives pli-vism exclusion, and the first-definitional work in Q6. PREDICTED for the control: pƒ´ti ratio (Q4) FLAT across all three layers and pƒ´ti defframe count (Q8) near-zero in every layer. The layer label itself is NOT independent evidence (T1: stratum() is a work_slug lookup); evidentiary weight is in the ratio SHAPE plus the blind-coded defining-frame first-appearance, cross-checked against the named primary loci.
primary_loci: MN 117 MahƒÅcattƒÅrƒ´saka: 'takko vitakko sa·πÖkappo appanƒÅ byappanƒÅ cetaso abhiniropanƒÅ vacƒ´sa·πÖkhƒÅro', the one canonical (mula) frame where abhiniropanƒÅ sits beside vitakka; critical test of whether the application reading is already canonical or merely list-adjacent
MN 19 DvedhƒÅvitakka + MN 20 Vitakkasa·πá·π≠hƒÅna: vitakka in the discursive/intentional field (two-sorts-of-thinking, stilling of thoughts), the mula DISCURSIVE anchor
MN 78 Sama·πáamu·πá·∏çika: vitakka/sa·πÖkappa as kusala/akusala thought, discursive-field locus
Dhammasa·πÖga·πái + AtthasƒÅlinƒ´ (Dhs-a ~157): the abhiniropana lifting-onto-the-object head-gloss; predicted FIRST definitional locus
Visuddhimagga IV.88 (pli-vism): vicƒÅra = anusa√±cara·πáa/anumajjana, vitakka = ƒÅramma·πáe abhiniropana; the systematizing commentary core
Pa·π≠isambhidƒÅmagga + Vibha·πÖga (jhƒÅna-vibha·πÖga): abhidhamma-layer transitional glossing of the jhƒÅna factors
scope_limits: Corpus-linguistic only: dates when a LEXICAL/DEFINITIONAL pattern appears in the held Pali text by layer; cannot settle the doctrinal question of what the Buddha meant by vitakka, nor adjudicate the thinking-vs-application interpretive debate.
stratum()/layer is a work_slug LOOKUP, not independent chronology (T1): mula vs attha is a textual-role partition, not a dated timeline; no absolute dating claims.
pli-vism is shelved as mula but treated as commentary here; results are reported both with and without it, and the convention choice is a known sensitivity, not a fact about chronology.
Foreign-language parallels (lzh/san) are graph-only (parallel_have=false); cannot check whether the application gloss has Sanskrit/Chinese Abhidharma analogues; structural mentions only.
Passage-presence counting cannot distinguish one definitional use from a passing mention without the blind sense-audit; pre-audit ratios are provisional.
CST coarse-vagga vs SC fine-sutta granularity makes distinct-works/suttas counts approximate; densities use is_primary dedup but passage size differs by edition.
iaa_plan: "Two independent coders (or two blinded agent passes under a frozen rubric) code the Q5 defining-frame candidate rows (TARGET + APPLICATION + DEFINING_FRAME co-hits) on ONE binary label: is abhiniropana/ƒÅropana functioning as a DEFINITIONAL head-noun glossing vitakka here (abhiniropana is PREDICATED of vitakka as its meaning/characteristic) versus a mere co-mention/list-item (e.g. the MN 117 synonym chain where abhiniropanƒÅ is one item, NOT a definition OF vitakka). Coders see original_head with work_slug/layer MASKED (layer-blind) to prevent layer-priming. Compute Cohen's kappa; require kappa >= 0.6 to proceed, adjudicate disagreements, and only adjudicated DEFINITIONAL rows feed Q6's first-appearance claim. A second kappa pass on a 50-row APPLICATION-sense sample (does the abhiniropana token mean mental application-to-object vs an unrelated sense of ƒÅropana such as planting/raising) guards the carita-lesson polysemy risk."
